steps.title: Identify the horizontal asymptote to find b
steps.content: For exponential functions of the form $y = a^x + b$, as $x \to -\infty$, the term $a^x \to 0$ (when $a > 1$), leaving only the constant $b$. The graph shows the curve approaching a horizontal asymptote at $y = -2$. Therefore, $b = -2$.

steps.title: Use a point on the curve to find a
steps.content:
The graph marks a point at $(1, 7)$. Substituting into $y = a^x + b$:
$$7 = a^1 + (-2)$$
$$7 = a - 2$$
$$a = 9$$
That matches the exponential behavior shown in the graph.
